commands.author: potterdigital
commands.command: re-opening request тАФ the close attribution doesn't hold up under file-level check. **issue with the cited fix.** pr #85500 ("recover stuck codex compaction") is the cited merged fix, but its `files` list on the github api only touches `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts`, `compact.test.ts`, and `changelog.md` (3 files, +178/-27). it does not modify `src/agents/harness/selection.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts`, `src/config/sessions/transcript.ts`, or `src/agents/model-fallback.ts` тАФ the files the review pointed to as fix locations. **selection guard isn't present on `main` either.** i diffed `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` between tag `v2026.5.20` and `main` at `304ff68c7975` тАФ the files are byte-identical. the `throw new missingagentharnesserror(runtime)` on the explicit-non-\`auto\` branch is still live on \`main\`, with no preceding guard for cli runtime aliases. the existing \`iscliagentruntime\`-based short-circuit lives in \`src/agents/model-fallback.ts\` (\`assertmodelfallbackcandidateharnessavailable\`), but that's the model-fallback pre-flight, not the harness selector that \`agent-runner-execution.ts\` and the auto-reply paths call. **production frequency.** on a v2026.5.20 install with one agent's default model set to \`agentruntime.id: \"claude-cli\"\`, the error fires every 1тАУ5 minutes against both \`lane=session:agent:<id>:slack:...\` and \`lane=session:agent:<id>:dashboard:...\` lanes (logged at \`subsystem-bivbrvcg.js:179\`). the slack \`[[reply_to:]]\` poisoning is one downstream effect; the dashboard lane swallows it without visible substitution. pre-existing context in the original issue body still applies (the delivered payload has \`provider: openclaw, model: gateway-injected\`, so it's gateway-synthesized, not model-emitted). **proposed fix.** mirror the existing \`runtime === \"codex\" && policy.runtimesource === \"implicit\" тЖТ return piharness\` escape hatch in \`selectagentharnessdecision()\`, but for the documented cli runtime aliases (\`claude-cli\`, \`codex-cli\`, \`gemini-cli\`). same \`selectedreason: \"implicit_plugin_unavailable_pi\"\` enum value, same shape, no new type. verified locally by patching the bundled \`dist/selection-bmjednna.js\` on a v2026.5.20 install тАФ the lane-task error has not re-fired in the 30+ minutes since restart. (caveat: i've only confirmed the throw stops; i have not separately confirmed the slack \`[[reply_to:]]\` delivery path now posts the actual model reply text rather than the warning. will follow up if the next slack interaction confirms.) happy to send a pr if that's useful.
commands.freeform_prompt: Re-opening request тАФ the close attribution doesn't hold up under file-level check. **Issue with the cited fix.** PR #85500 ("Recover stuck Codex compaction") is the cited merged fix, but its `files` list on the GitHub API only touches `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts`, `compact.test.ts`, and `CHANGELOG.md` (3 files, +178/-27). It does not modify `src/agents/harness/selection.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts`, `src/config/sessions/transcript.ts`, or `src/agents/model-fallback.ts` тАФ the files the review pointed to as fix locations. **Selection guard isn't present on `main` either.** I diffed `src/agents/harness/selection.ts` between tag `v2026.5.20` and `main` at `304ff68c7975` тАФ the files are byte-identical. The `throw new MissingAgentHarnessError(runtime)` on the explicit-non-\`auto\` branch is still live on \`main\`, with no preceding guard for CLI runtime aliases. The existing \`isCliAgentRuntime\`-based short-circuit lives in \`src/agents/model-fallback.ts\` (\`assertModelFallbackCandidateHarnessAvailable\`), but that's the model-fallback pre-flight, not the harness selector that \`agent-runner-execution.ts\` and the auto-reply paths call. **Production frequency.** On a v2026.5.20 install with one agent's default model set to \`agentRuntime.id: \"claude-cli\"\`, the error fires every 1тАУ5 minutes against both \`lane=session:agent:<id>:slack:...\` and \`lane=session:agent:<id>:dashboard:...\` lanes (logged at \`subsystem-BIvbRvCg.js:179\`). The Slack \`[[reply_to:]]\` poisoning is one downstream effect; the dashboard lane swallows it without visible substitution. Pre-existing context in the original issue body still applies (the delivered payload has \`provider: openclaw, model: gateway-injected\`, so it's gateway-synthesized, not model-emitted). **Proposed fix.** Mirror the existing \`runtime === \"codex\" && policy.runtimeSource === \"implicit\" тЖТ return piHarness\` escape hatch in \`selectAgentHarnessDecision()\`, but for the documented CLI runtime aliases (\`claude-cli\`, \`codex-cli\`, \`gemini-cli\`). Same \`selectedReason: \"implicit_plugin_unavailable_pi\"\` enum value, same shape, no new type. Verified locally by patching the bundled \`dist/selection-BmjEdnnA.js\` on a v2026.5.20 install тАФ the lane-task error has not re-fired in the 30+ minutes since restart. (Caveat: I've only confirmed the throw stops; I have not separately confirmed the Slack \`[[reply_to:]]\` delivery path now posts the actual model reply text rather than the warning. Will follow up if the next Slack interaction confirms.) Happy to send a PR if that's useful.

target.title: Gateway delivery layer treats `claude-cli` CLI runtime id as agent harness, fails closed on Slack [[reply_to:]] path
target.author: potterdigital

commands.reason: author association NONE and repository permission read are not allowed
